untranslatable_reason: Signal requires macro regime identification, conviction level assessment, and options-specific leverage mechanics which cannot be expressed using price-based technical indicators. Macro regime inflection, trader conviction thresholds, and options strategy selection require external regime classification and portfolio-level logic outside the scope of indicator-based rules. Capital deployment percentage (90-100%) and 300%+ profit targets are position-sizing parameters, not tradeable conditions.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy translation using trend confirmation (ADX > 25) + downtrend (price < SMA50) as a simplified bearish regime identifier, with downtrend reversal as exit. However, this fundamentally misses the signal's core requirement: macro regime identification followed by leveraged capital concentration into single-instrument classes. The original signal is strategic/portfolio-level, not tactical/indicator-level. Backtest engine cannot execute without: (1) external macro regime input, (2) conviction scoring mechanism, (3) options contract specification, (4) leverage/margin configuration.
